title: deschampsia cespitosa subsp. paludosa
intro: Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. paludosa, also known as wet wood meadow-grass or palustre hair grass, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. This grass is native to wet meadows and marshes, particularly in the British Isles and Europe. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. paludosa is typically prepared as a herbal infusion or tincture to harness its health benefits, including soothing skin irritations and promoting wound healing.
